***ACG Cheat Sheet***  
[**Camera Offline**](#bookmark=id.yi4qh9qx433h)  
[**Bridge Offline**](#bookmark=id.ytcey59cjl8f)  
[**Updates**](#bookmark=id.6lqhzlaef7hi)  
[**Switch Issues**](#bookmark=id.umx7bgs0akoq)  
[**Misc. Information**](#bookmark=id.kbzrty2lquqm)

### Camera Issues:

* **Camera Offline**  
  * Pingable? Tunnel into it?  
  * [JJ's Offline Camera T/S](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1eFTa6DGIN2vrYHCNz7CRCMwqmkj_aXDPn_aTnFsx54k/edit#heading=h.nxwmebv1m6bm)  
  * Check CamLAN NIC is up

**ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.17.5;ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=smokeping \--version=2.7.3-10;ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=camera-support \--version=v20231101**

* Why is the cam offline??

| F '{Cam ESN}|{CAM GUID}|{CAM MAC}|{CAM IP}' 2\>/dev/null |
| :---- |

* **Cam in HOLD status**  
  * Is it attached? Does it have an ESN?  
    * cd /opt/een/etc/bridge  
    * touch bridge\_local.conf  
    * vim bridge\_local.con  
    * bridgevalidation=true  
      * restart bridge  
* **When was current settings last applied?**  
  * cd /opt/een/var/bridge/esns/\<esn\>  
  * cd /opt/een/var/bridge/esns/100f22ca  
  * cat settings | grep retention  
    * looking for ‘bornonts’  
      * “Born on timestamp”

### Bridge Issues:

* **Cert error (check cookie often)**  
  * python3 fix\_bridge\_certs.py \<esn\> \<cookie, from admin\>  
  * python3 fix\_bridge\_certs.py 100874b4 c001\~ece5f299d37beff3bd93048bcc654cce  
  * C001\~ca4280351fa2a362e93461dfa7a1ab56  
  * python fix\_bridge\_certs.py \<your\_esn\> \<eenadmin\_cookie\>  
  * To get that cookie. Go to eenadmin, and in Inspect → Application → Cookies, pick the value of vbsadmin\_sessionid  
* To get to host, ssh root@localhost \-p 33022  
* **Bridge offline**  
  * Check WAN/CamLAN  
  * Run Network diagnostics  
* **Run custom network diagnostic**  
  * you can make your own network diagnostic:  
  * have reseller change WAN from DHCP to static (don't save)  
  * take note of bridge IP (if any)  
  * have him ping that IP from local network (laptop, etc...)’  
* **How many analytics on bridge**  
  * ps aux | grep \-e 'analog.\*analytics' | grep \-v grep | wc \-l  
    * To see esn of camera  
      * ps aux | grep \-e 'analog.\*analytics' | grep \-v grep  
* **gst-streamer.log backtrace (WIP)**  
  *  Note the timestamp from gst-streamer logs  
  *  Check which cameras were active at that time

```shell
  for dir in /opt/een/data/var/bridge/cameras/*/; do
      uuid=$(basename "$dir")
      ip=$(grep -oP '@\K[0-9.]+' "$dir/stream_info.json" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
      esn=$(grep -l "$uuid" /opt/een/data/var/bridge/esns/*/trace_log.json 2>/dev/null | cut -d'/' -f8)
      echo "UUID: $uuid | ESN: $esn | IP: $ip"
  done
```

  * ls \-la /opt/een/data/var/bridge/cameras/  
  * cat /opt/een/data/var/bridge/cameras/\*/stream\_info.json | grep \-A5 "video\_url"  
  *  Now you can correlate gst-streamer logs back to specific cameras\! Problem solved\!   
* **Bridge Health**  
  * speedtest (while in container)  
    * speedtest \--secure  
  * ipccli \--bridge\_diag \--long  
  * df \-h  
  * Check disc reads  
    * yum install atop \-y && atop | grep avio , watch for greater than 35.0  
  * In [supportool](https://support.aus1hub1.eencloud.com/), check temp, cpu average, mem virtual, uptime (process of bridge running),   
  * Journalctl  
    * journalctl \-S 2025-27-10 \-U 2025-31-10 \--no-pager | grep ‘waiting for smokeping to stop’  
    * journalctl \-x | grep “waiting for smokeping to stop”  
    * journalctl \-S "2025-10-27 02:02:00 UTC" \-U "2025-10-27 02:04:00 UTC" \--no-pager  
  * dmesg | grep segfault  
  * dmesg \--level=err  
  * Fan speed  
    * from the container, run: yum install lm\_sensors \-y  
    * say yes to everything   
    * then run: sensors-detect  
    * say yes to everything  
    * exit to host  
    * Run: sensors-detect  
    * say yes to everything again   
    * then run: sensors  
  * Fan Speed (Kulina updated 2024\)  
    * sensors-detect \--auto  
    * cat /etc/sensors3.conf | grep \-i fan  
    * sensors | grep \-i fan  
  * Dirtydmesg  
    * segfaults  
  * Journalerr  
    * Coredumps  
  * Check raid serials  
    * for i in /dev/sd?; do echo $i:; smartctl \-a $i | egrep '(Model|Serial|Capacity)'; done  
* **Missing Wan/CamLAN**  
  * nmcli con show  
  * nmcli con up camlan0 or nmcli con up wan0  
  * NO CAMLAN ON BRIDGE (in host)  
    * 1\. upgrader \--udev  
    * 2\. personalityctl commit  
    * 3\. Reboot  
  * switch fix  
    * Ipccli –fix\_mswitch –iface=’camlan0’  
    *   
* **DMESG Error**  
  * dmesg \--level=err  
* netstat \-ant  
* **Journalerr**  
  * journalctl \-p err \-b \-o short-precise \--no-pager  
* **Unable to default camera?**  
  * oshell \-u \<user\> \-p \<password\> \<IP\> \-c 'print onvif.device.service.SetSystemFactoryDefault("Soft")'  
  * oshell \-u admin \-p 5074611042 10.143.93.151 \-c 'print onvif.device.service.SetSystemFactoryDefault("Soft")'  
  * oshell \-u i3admin \-p i3admin 192.0.0.80  
* ONVIF  
  * oshell \-u admin \-p 5074611042 10.143.93.151 \-c 'print onvif.device.service.GetNetworkProtocols()'  
  * oshell \-u admin \-p 'Security12' 110.143.145.242 \-c 'print onvif.device.service.GetNetworkProtocols()'  
  * oshell \-u admin \-p S3cur1ty\! 10.143.166.232  \-c 'print onvif.device.service.GetNetworkProtocols()'  
  *   
* **Look for assets**  
  * `x`  
    * cd /opt/een/data/bridge/assets; df \-h .; du \-sh $(ls) | sort \--human-numeric-sort  
    * du \-sh $(ls)  
    * Output into data by year/month/day  
      echo \-e "CameraID\\tYear\\tMonth\\tSize" && \\  
      for CAMERA\_DIR in \*/; do \\  
          if \[ \-d "$CAMERA\_DIR" \]; then \\  
              CAMERA\_NAME=$(basename "$CAMERA\_DIR"); \\  
              for YEAR\_DIR in "$CAMERA\_DIR"/\*/; do \\  
                  if \[ \-d "$YEAR\_DIR" \]; then \\  
                      YEAR\_NAME=$(basename "$YEAR\_DIR"); \\  
                      if compgen \-G "$YEAR\_DIR/\[0-9\]\[0-9\]" \> /dev/null; then \\  
                          for MONTH\_DIR in "$YEAR\_DIR"/\[0-9\]\[0-9\]/; do \\  
                              if \[ \-d "$MONTH\_DIR" \]; then \\  
                                  MONTH\_NAME=$(basename "$MONTH\_DIR"); \\  
                                  SIZE=$(du \-sh "$MONTH\_DIR" | awk '{print $1}'); \\  
                                  echo \-e "$CAMERA\_NAME\\t$YEAR\_NAME\\t$MONTH\_NAME\\t$SIZE"; \\  
                              fi; \\  
                          done; \\  
                      else \\  
                          SIZE=$(du \-sh "$YEAR\_DIR" | awk '{print $1}'); \\  
                          echo \-e "$CAMERA\_NAME\\t$YEAR\_NAME\\tTotal\\t$SIZE"; \\  
                      fi; \\  
                  fi; \\  
              done; \\  
          fi; \\  
      done  
        
      *   
* **Make a brick??**  
  * Add :brick::brick to camlan0 or wan0 after ifconfig to make the brick, then add ip address  
    * ifconfig camlan0:brick 10.143.0.1Take it down when you are done (or will disappear on reboot)  
    * ifconfig camlan0:brick down  
  * Can also specify the netmask  
    * ifconfig camlan0:brick 192.168.1.1 netmask 255.255.0.0  
* Check send/recieve on bridge?  
  * iftop \-i wan0  
    * To display received traffic only in the iftop command, you can use the t key to toggle between different display modes: two lines per host, one line per host, received traffic only, and sent traffic only.  
* **Bridge logs**  
  * cd /opt/een/var/log/bridge  
    * tail \-f bridge.log | grep ptz  
  * 

**Updates:** 

* [**Current FW Versions**](https://eenadmin.eagleeyenetworks.com/eenadmin/bridge_update_dashboard/)  
* **Plat1 to Plat2 Upgrade**  
  * [Plat 1 to Plat 2](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/2744352788/Bridge+Applications+Upgrading+ancient+versions)

**Plat 1**  
	yum install \-y upgrader ipc python-diag  
yum install \--enablerepo=een \-y update-to bridge-product-2.4.4  
yum update-to bridge-product-2.0.14 \-y (if it’s extra old)

* yum upgrade-to bridge-product-2.4.4 \-y  
* yum clean all; yum install \-y dns-manager upgrader ipc python-diag  
* sed \-i 's/mirrorlist/\#mirrorlist/g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-\* &&  sed \-i 's|\#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.centos.org|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-\*  
* yum clean all && yum install \--disablerepo=\* \--enablerepo=een \-y upgrader ipc python-diag && upgrader \--train=prod \--everything

Plat 1 curlerror

* yum \--disablerepo=\* \--enablerepo=een update-to bridge-product-2.4.4 \-y

Bridge Health

* bridge-diag \--long

Jake commands:

* ​​P1 \- yum clean all && yum install upgrader && upgrader train=prod \--everything  
* P1 \- yum \--disablerepo=\* \--enablerepo=een install \-y upgrader

* P2 \- dnf clean all && dnf install \-y upgrader ipc python-diag && upgrader train=prod \--everything

   

* **Plat 2 (MAKE SURE IT IS NOT DNFW)**  
  * **Run FIRST**  
    * **yum install \-y upgrader ipc python-diagf**  
  * Get bridge app/host os  
    * ipccli \--get\_host\_os && ipccli \--get\_container  
  * HOST OS UPDATE  
    * che  
    * Host OS Update  
      * yum install \-y upgrader ipc python-diag && upgrader \--osver=2.0.0-6 \--everything  
  * Bridge App Update  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.17  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.19.3  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.22.3  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.24.1  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.25.3  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.26.1  
  * Smokeping Update  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=smokeping \--version=2.7.3-10  
  * Cam Support Update  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=camera-support \--version=v20251208  
  * LPR update  
    * yum install \-y ipc python-diag && ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=ee-lpr-application-init \--version=2.2.0  
  * Zero-reboot  
    * ipccli \--add\_container \--container=zero-reboot \--version=1.6.1-20250821 \--url=c000.bridge.eencloud.com/bridge/zero-reboot  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=zero-reboot \--version=1.6.1-20250821  
  * RTSP  
    * ipccli \--add\_container \--container=bridge-rtsp-app \--version=1.5.0  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge-rtsp-app \--version=1.5.0  
    * \--url=$(cat/opt/een/etc/bridge/bridge.cluster).bridge.eencloud.com/bridge/bridge-rtsp-app  
  * TalkDown  
    * Install TalkDown  
      * ipccli \--add\_container \--container=talkdown \--version=1.15.0 \--url=$(cat /opt/een/etc/bridge/bridge.cluster).bridge.eencloud.com/bridge/talkdown-client  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=talkdown \--version=1.15.0  
  * Remove  
  * Kernel  
    * ipccli \--get\_host\_kernel  
    * ipccli \--get\_available\_kernels  
    * upgrader \--kernver=5.15.74  
  * A Jake update??  
    * dnf install \-y upgrader ipc python-diag && upgrader \--traim=prod \--everything  
  * All Containers Update  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=bridge \--version=3.17.0 && ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=smokeping \--version=2.7.3-10 && ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--container=camera-support \--version=v20230816  
    * ipccli \--upgrade\_container \--version=3.15.3 && watch " ipccli \--get\_container"  
  * List containers  
    * ipccli \--get\_host\_os | grep hostos;  ipccli \--get\_host\_kernel | grep \-v Sent | grep kernel; ipccli \--list\_containers | grep \-E '\["\]:|config|active'  
    * ipccli \--get\_host\_os | grep hostos; ipccli \--list\_containers | grep \-E '\["\]:|config|active'  
  * Is upgrade running? Check through exec?  
    * ps ax | grep upgrade  
  * NO IPC Socket  
    * systemctl restart een-ipc  
  * NO MIRROR LIST ISSUE  
    * cd /etc/yum.repos.d/ &&  sed \-i 's/mirrorlist/\#mirrorlist/g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-\* &&  sed \-i 's|\#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.centos.org|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-\* && yum install \-y upgrader ipc python-diag  
    * THEN yum install \-y upgrader ipc python-diag && upgrader \--osver=2022-07-19 \--everything  
    * THEN yum install \-y upgrader ipc python-diag && upgrader \--train=prod \--everything  
  * Check update status  
    * tail \-F /var/log/platform-upgrader.log  2\>/dev/null  
  * Stop upgrade  
    * upgrader \--clear  
  * Access cred files for cameras  
    * cat /opt/een/var/bridge/security/credentials.js  
  * Check for carrier  
    * ip link | grep \-E 'wan|camlan'  
  * CentOS- does not exist…  
    * touch CentOS-\*  
    * cd /etc/yum.repos.d/ &&  sed \-i 's/mirrorlist/\#mirrorlist/g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-\* &&  sed \-i 's|\#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.centos.org|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-\* && yum reinstall \--disablerepo=\* \--enablerepo=een upgrader \-y ipc python-diag

### Switch Issues:

* **Cams offline/no switch/’lost’ in CamTool**  
  * nmcli con show  
  * nmcli con up camlan0  
  * nmcli dev status  
* **Camdriver**  
  * python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-mi \-a \<IP of the switch\> \-p \<password of the switch\>  
  * python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-mi \-a 10.143.109.141  \-p 9256989901			  
* **Not available to add**  
  * Plug MAC address into [Manufactured Devices](https://eenadmin.eagleeyenetworks.com/eenadmin/vms_admin/), grab root password, add to camera locker (under Account Settings \> Camera)  
    * If fails, telnet with IP and ‘loaddefault’  
      * telnet \<switch IP\> , admin/\<grab password from camtool\>  
* **Find switch info in host**  
  * f  
  * docker exec \-it bridge-bridge-1 python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-d  
    * docker exec \-it bridge\_bridge\_1 python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-d  
  * docker exec \-it bridge\_bridge\_1 python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-d  
  * python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-mi \-a \<IP of the switch\> \-p \<password of the switch\>  
    * python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-mi \-a 10.143.181.162 \-p @EagleAdmin23  
  * REBOOT G SWITCH  
    * python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-i \--reboot \-a 10.143.181.162 \-p @EagleAdmin23  
      * python \-m camdriver.eenswitch \-ic \-a 10.143.181.162 \-p @EagleAdmin23

**Check for USB:**

* dmesg | grep \-vE 'iptables|usb usb|hub hub|usb 2-1|hub 2-1|usb 1-2'  
* cat /proc/bus/input/devices/\*/manufacturer

**Misc. Stuff:**

* **Duplicating GUIDs??**  
  * **Checks for amount of guids**  
    * ls /opt/een/var/bridge/cameras| wc \-l  
  * **Watch list for amount of guids… is number increasing?**  
    * watch "ls /opt/een/var/bridge/cameras | wc \-l"  
  * **Clean multiplying GUIDS (into directory, creates list of guids assigned to esn, lists out assigned guids/esn, deletes non assigned guids)**  
    * cd /opt/een/var/bridge/esns  
    * for i in \*/camera\_service\_status; do echo \-n "$i: "; strings $i | head \-2 | tail \-1; done | awk \-F'\[/:\]' '{print $3}' | awk \-F' \+' '{print $2"|"}'\> /rmgtest  
    * cat /rmgtest  
    * cd /opt/een/var/bridge/cameras  
    * for i in $(ls | grep \-vE "$(cat /rmgtest | tr \-d '\[:space:\]' | sed 's/.$//')"); do rm \-rf $i; done  
* **Need local display via monitor?**   
  * microdnf install ImageMagick  
* **IP Conflict?**  
  * arping \-b \-I camlan0 \<ip\>  
  * arping \-b \-I  wan0  10.21.114.182  
* **Negotiated Port Speeds**  
  * ethtool \<wan0/camlan0/camlan1\>  
  * ethtool wan0  
* **Camera Dropping Streams?**  
  * tail \-F /opt/een/var/log/bridge/streamer.log | grep \<IP\>  
  * tail \-f /opt/een/var/log/bridge/streamer.log | grep 10.14.0.90  
* **Check raid drives**  
  * for i in /dev/sd?; do echo $i:; smartctl \-a $i | egrep '(Model|Serial|Capacity)'; done  
* **Analytic Preview**  
  * Analytic preview asks for 16 FPS, will give error message if camera can’t handle  
    * Camera has to be able to do at least 8 FPS or analytic preview won’t work  
    * The more FPS the more BW the camera will need  
  * 4fps for intrusion  
  * 8/12 fps for line crossing  
  * 16 fps for counting  
* **Firewall**  
  * nmap \-sT \-sU \-T25 \-p 80,443,773,8081,8082,50000,60000,554 \<ip\> | grep \-ivE '80/u|443/u|773/u|8081/u|8082/t|50000/u|60000/u|554/u'  
    * nmap \-sT \-sU \-T25 \-p 80,443,773,8081,8082,50000,60000,8554 167.94.38.5 | grep \-ivE '80/u|443/u|773/u|8081/u|8082/t|50000/u|60000/u|8554/u’  
    * nmap \-sT \-p 80,443,773,8081,8082,50000,60000 216.245.90.25  
  * netstat \-ant | egrep "FIN|SYN"  
    * Followed by nmap \-p \<port that fails netstat\> \<IP that fails netstat\>

* **Onvif Discovery (on CamLAN)**  
  * python \-m camdriver.discovery \-a \<CamLAN IP\>  
    * python \-m camdriver.discovery \-a 10.143.0.1  
    * python \-m camdriver.discovery \-a 10.119.41.254  
    * python \-m camdriver.discovery \-a 10.237.1.4  
    * \-d \= unicast  
      * python \-m camdriver.discovery \-a 10.10.0.219 \-d 10.10.8.111  
* **DHCP Discover**  
  * nmap \--script broadcast-dhcp-discover  
* **Warranty**  
  * Bridges- lifetime warranty  
  * Everything else (including CMVRs)- **two years**  
* **Display Station**  
  * From Ionodes: [https://www.ionodes.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/IONODES-ION-R200-User-Manual.pdf](https://www.ionodes.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/IONODES-ION-R200-User-Manual.pdf)  
    * [https://docs.google.com/document/d/16BqlQNoBbuzxeGuQwrEx2xUjLZUXU0LIz-LWuJR7zd0/edit](https://docs.google.com/document/d/16BqlQNoBbuzxeGuQwrEx2xUjLZUXU0LIz-LWuJR7zd0/edit)  
    * [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ku6nGq4khiWySVYSq52a92T1pBKy0Q\_\_YiH2nERnnhE/edit](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ku6nGq4khiWySVYSq52a92T1pBKy0Q__YiH2nERnnhE/edit)  
    * [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qVe49EgxRK-f4tkxzE-W0ie-s3BrnkpM4ihD93zNKWE/edit](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qVe49EgxRK-f4tkxzE-W0ie-s3BrnkpM4ihD93zNKWE/edit)  
  * 1c697aa765f4  
  * 1c:69:7a:a7:77:49  
  * 1c697a6abc3e  
  * 1C:69 or start with 48:XX  
  * 00:07 for **DS100**  
  * admin/@EagleAdmin23 (this is shareable\!\!\!\!\!\!)  
* **Default IP for Eagle Eye Cameras**  
  * 192.168. 0.120 or 192.168.0.121  
    * Supposedly ship with DHCP enabled though, so no IP is technically set  
* **Can’t tunnel into bridge**  
  * **Plat 2?**  
    * **yum \--disablerepo=\* \--enablerepo=een clean all && yum reinstall \--disablerepo=\* \--enablerepo=een \-y openssh-clients openssh openssh-server**  
  * yum install \-y openssh openssh-server  
* **SSH into HOST**  
  * ssh mfg@localhost \-p 33022  
    * Eagle23soaring  
    * een\<last5ofserialBACKWARDS\>  
    * EEN\<last5ofserialBACKWARDS\>  
* **SSH into Root**  
  * ssh root@localhost \-p 33022  
* **SSH into offline bridge, from 2nd offline bridge**  
  * Find bridges on network  
    * nmap \-oG \- \-p 33022 $(ip route | grep \-v default | grep \-E 'swan|eth1' | awk \-F ' \+' '{ print $1}') | grep open  
  * ssh root@10.1.10.253 \-p 33022  
  * ra\!3@KNS3pMi  
  * 192.168.10.1  
  * Bridge config  
    * ssh admin@172.16.0.241  
  * ssh root@192.168.1.98 \-p 33022  
    * Don’t need 33022 for plat1  
* **Check to see if password works**  
  * ssh admin@localhost  
* **ECHO ip into GUID**  
  * echo "http://\<camera ip\>/onvif/device\_service" \> last\_address  
  * echo "http://10.143.39.26/onvif/device\_service" \> last\_address

* **EXEC from browser \- no support pin needed**  
  * http://\<ARCHIVER\>.eagleeyenetworks.com:28080/execute.html?c=\<ESN\>  
  * http://\<ESN\>.a.plumv.com:28080/execute.html?c=\<ESN\>  
  * http://1005e115.a.plumv.com:28080/execute.html?c=1005e115  
* **HIKVISION HTML/XML error**  
  * make sure onvif user is same as admin username and admin password, and that onvif user is also an administrator  
  * Time is accurate.  
  * RTSP authentication is either Digest or off.  
  * Illegal login lock is not enabled.  
  * Change onvif to ws-token  
* **Log check for updates**  
  * journalctl \-u een-ipc \--no-pager \-S “today”  
  * watch "ipccli \--get\_container;ipccli \--get\_container \--container=ee-lpr-application-init"  
  * journalctl \--no-pager | grep upgrade\_container  
* **Resolve WHATs on the network**  
  * nmap \-sP \-n \[ip\]/subnet range  
    * nmap \-sP \-n 10.143.0.1/16  
    * Arp  
  * Nmap on Wan  
    * nmap \-n \-sP $(ip route | grep \-E " wan0 proto kernel|eth1  proto kernel"  | awk \-F ' \+' '{ print $1}')  
  * Nmap on CamLAN  
    * nmap \-n \-sP $(ip route | grep \-E " camlan0 proto kernel|eth0  proto kernel"  | awk \-F ' \+' '{ print $1}')  
  *   
* **Missing/incorrect GUID**  
  * cd /opt/een/var/bridge/cameras  
  * cd GUID (doesn't exist)  
  * mkdir GUID  
  * cd GUID  
  * touch last \_address  
  * echo "http://10.143.225.119/onvif/device\_service" \> last\_address  
  * restart all from container  
* **FPS Bridge Sees Camera Have?**  
  * ls \-la /opt/een/data/var/bridge/esns/10032e10/  
  * cat /opt/een/data/var/bridge/esns/10032e10/trace\_log.json  
  * cat /opt/een/data/var/bridge/cameras/3a7f9ce0-b716-11ef-8f57-001c27251849/stream\_info.json | python \-m json.tool  
  * IN CONTAINER  
    * H264: ffprobe "rtsp://admin:9117307221@172.23.28.17:554/snl/live/1/1" 2\>&1 | grep Stream  
    * Preview: ffprobe "rtsp://admin:Dy3zzpsw18%21@10.143.36.44/media/video2" 2\>&1 | grep Stream  
      * ffmpeg \-i "rtsp://admin:9117307221@172.23.28.17:554/snl/live/1/1" \-t 10 \-f null \- 2\>&1 | grep "frame="  
* **Camera PEND but not showing as available to add**  
  * F12 \> search device  
* **Retention Settings**  
  * for i in /opt/een/var/bridge/esns/\*/settings;do echo $i | awk \-F/ '{print $7}';python \-m json.tool $i | grep \-iE 'bridge|reten' | sort;done  
* **Check for purge?**  
  * python \-m json.tool /opt/een/var/log/bridge/purge.json  
* **Ping All IPs 50 packets**  
  * In container  
    * Sshblocal  
    * Prepfping  
    * Getpingli  
    * Pingshortlist or pinglist  
  * yum install fping \-y && arp \-an | awk \-F'\[()\]' '{print $2}' | head \-100 | grep 192.168 \> /test && cat /test && fping \-c 50 \-p 1000 \-f /test  
  * yum install fping \-y && arp \-an | awk \-F'\[()\]' '{print $2}' | head \-100 | grep 10.143 \> /test && cat /test && fping \-c 50 \-p 1000 \-f /test  
  * **Oct 2025:** arp \-an | awk '/10.143./ {print $2}' | tr \-d '()' | xargs \-I {} \-P 0 ping \-c 25 {}  
* **ARP history**  
  * cat /proc/net/arp  
* **Cam Tool values are:**  
  * • STRM \== Streaming  
  * • PEND \== Pending and driver is built, waiting for user to add camera  
  * • HOLD \== did not match a driver, need to build driver  
  * • NONE \== need password or could not find driver  
  * • PASS  \== password required  
  * • CONF \== in ONVIF configuration state machine  
  * • CERR \== Configuration Error  
  * • ERR    \== camera is in an error state, need to look at username/password, firmware, connection  
  * • FOND  \== ask jeff camera ip  
  * • COFF  \== Camera Off  
  * • NSIG  \==  Analog camera  
* **Wrong time on localx**  
  * curl \-s "http://\<ESN\>.a.plumv.com:28080/camera/command?t=settings\_get;c=\<ESN\>" | jq .user\_settings.settings.timezone "US/Central"  
  * curl \-s "http://\<ESN\>.a.plumv.com:28080/camera/command?t=settings\_get;c=\<ESN\>" | jq .user\_settings.settings.timezone "US/Central"  
* **Check Resolution of Monitor used for local display**  
  * docker exec \-it bridge\_bridge\_1 grep 'initial mode' /var/log/Xorg.0.log  
* **Local X command ??**  
  * ps ax | grep xplayer  
* **TUNNEL ISSUES**  
  * Unlock the SubAccount with the Reseller info.  
  * Proctor the Bridge for any errors  
  * Open Exec and run df \-h.  
  * If the Overlay is 100% then reboot the unit to clear this.  
  * Use the Open Tunnel button to create a link and paste that into your Terminal.  
* **Tunnel into second offline bridge on same network**  
  * ssh root@\<ip address\> \-p 33022  
  * ssh root@10.5.193.24 \-p 33022  
* **Checking if download is going**  
  * Go into Debug Mode \> F12 \> Look for “uinotification” \> Response \>  CTL \+ F the task ID  
    * Looking for ‘download URL’ / ‘preview’ /   
* **Raid**  
  * cat /proc/mdstat  
* **IP conflict?**  
  * arping \-b \-I camlan0 \<ip\>  
  * arping \-b \-I wan0 10.100.4.180  
* **Accounts**  
  * Sphere- 108973  
  * Antetokounbros \- 00141263  
* **Power Supply**  
  * EN-RP-003 Eagle Eye 304/306/224/324 Power Supply (19v)  
  * EN-RP-004 Eagle Eye 305/225/325 Power Supply (19v/48v)  
  * EN-RP-005 Eagle Eye 224+/3x4+/3x6+/4x4+ Power Supply (12v/36w)  
* **Find things on network**  
  * nmap \-sP \-n \[IP/rage\]  
  * nmap \-sP \-n 192.168.200.5/24  
* **Check For Presence of Cam**  
  * grep "100aaef9.\*presence" /opt/een/data/var/log/bridge/bridge.log  
* **Analog**  
  * for i in \`seq 1 16\`; do echo \-n " $i: " ; v4l2-ctl \--all \-d /dev/video\`expr $i \- 1\` | grep omposite; done  
  * If that doesn’t work, yum install v4l-utils \-y && for i in \`seq 1 16\`; do echo \-n " $i: " ; v4l2-ctl \--all \-d /dev/video\`expr $i \- 1\` | grep omposite; done  
  * If THAT doesn’t work, yum install  \-y v4l-utils  
* **If download doesn’t work**  
  * [https://login.eagleeyenetworks.com/asset/play/video.mp4?id=\<ESN\>\&start\_timestamp=\<Start](https://login.eagleeyenetworks.com/asset/play/video.mp4?id=%3CESN%3E&start_timestamp=%3CStart) timestamp UTC\>\&end\_timestamp=\<end\_timestamp in UTC\>  
  * https://login.eagleeyenetworks.com/asset/play/video.mp4?id=1008e304\&start\_timestamp=20240813174953.264\&end\_timestamp=20240813175540.764  
* **SupportCTL upgrade**  
  * sudo apt update && sudo apt dist-upgrade \-y  
*   
* **Test  for notifications**  
  * https://api.\<cluster\>.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/accounts/\<account\>/notifications  
  * https://api.c027.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/accounts/00155931/notifications?includeV1Notifications=true  
  * https://api.c000.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/accounts/00001081/notifications  
  * https://api.c005.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/accounts/00024007/jobs  
* **Motion alert not generating?**  
  * **https://apidocsold.eagleeyenetworks.com/\#event-objects**  
  * Check API  
    * [https://api](https://api).c006.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/accounts/00181603/notifications  
  * Check LUA  
    * http://\<esn\>.a.plumv.com:28080/lqservices.html  
    * http://100772da.a.plumv.com:28080/lqservices.html

![][image1]

* Check bridge  
  * Check logs  
    * ROME, ROMS, ALRS, ALRE

| 20240723204635.909:10010183 \- sending ALRS(motion\_2375981378) metric20240723204635.909:10010183 \- sending ALRS(motion\_2375981378) metric20240723204635.909:10010183 \- sending ALRS(motion\_2375981378) metric20240723204635.909:10010183 \- sending ALRS(motion\_2375981378) metric20240723204635.909:10010183 \- sending ALRS(motion\_2375981378) metric20240723204635.909:10010183 \- sending ALRS(motion\_2375981378) metric20240723204635.909:10010183 \- sending ALRS(motion\_2375981378) metric |
| :---- |

* **MTR for testing connections from exec by specific port number (run in exec)**  
  * **Can’t connect to bridge? Is it firewall? RUN THIS TO DENY THEIR CLAIMS**  
  * mtr \<working bridge archiver IP\> \-rbz \-T \-P \<port\>  
  * mtr 208.81.96.120 \-rbz \-T \-P 443  
* **Is footage in the cloud?**  
  * http://\<esn\>.a.plumv.com:28080/video.html?c=\<esn\>  
  * http://1001f32f.a.plumv.com:28080/video.html?c=1001f32f  
  * https://cnet.eagleeyenetworks.com/\#/camera\_history/10027948/20250604114446.579  
  * 20251004130214.400  
  * http://1001f32f.a.plumv.com:28080/video.html?c=1001f32f\&t=20250912120642.898\&count=-5  
* What footage is on archiver?  
  *   
* **Mstar**  
  * **[Upgrading the Mstar/Novatec Cams to latest FW](https://docs.google.com/document/d/162YdXx1umQyx6I_1Vn7O_VsNSx-_7kqT3F63En_U4z8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.3z5tpdlnzno)**  
  * Connect  
    * supportctl connect \<ESN\> \--put \~/new\_ee\_cam\_fw/mstar/t3.6.1303.1004.88.1.8.2.4\_20231107 \--target /opt/een/data/t3.6.1303.1004.88.1.8.2.4\_20231107  
    * supportctl connect 100a6984 \--put \~/new\_ee\_cam\_fw/mstar/t3.6.1303.1004.88.1.8.2.4\_20231107 \--target /opt/een/data/t3.6.1303.1004.88.1.8.2.4\_20231107  
    * **FOR MAC**  
      * ./supportctl connect 100d318a \--put \~/new\_ee\_cam\_fw/mstar/t3.6.1303.1004.88.1.8.2.4\_20231107 \--target /opt/een/data/t3.6.1303.1004.88.1.8.2.4\_20231107  
  * Update Nova4MP  
    * supportctl connect \<ESN\> \--put \~/NOVA4MP/v3.6.1602.1006.88.1.18.6.11.D03\_20240508 \--target /opt/een/data/v3.6.1602.1006.88.1.18.6.11.D03\_20240508  
    * supportctl connect 100c9a3c \--put \~/NOVA4MP/v3.6.1602.1006.88.1.18.6.11.D03\_20240508 \--target /opt/een/data/v3.6.1602.1006.88.1.18.6.11.D03\_20240508  
    * v3.6.1602.1006.88.1.18.6.11.D03\_20240508   
    * Update specific camera, must be in container  
      * python \-m camdriver.onvif.sunell \-a \<ip of camera\> \-u \<username of camera\> \-p \<password of camera\> \-f /opt/een/data/v3.6.1602.1006.88.1.18.6.11.D03\_20240508  
      * python \-m camdriver.onvif.sunell \-a 10.143.194.32 \-u admin \-p 9953929376 \-f /opt/een/data/v3.6.1603.1006.88.1.18.20.5.D01\_20240521  
      *   
  * Upload FW to cameras  
    * for i in $(cd /opt/een/var/bridge/cameras; cat \*/device\_config | strings | grep EN\\-....\\-...\\-2 \-A2 | grep '\\-001c'); do echo \-e "screen \-d \-m python \-m camdriver.onvif.sunell \-a $(cat /opt/een/var/bridge/cameras/$i/last\_address | grep \-v 169.254. | awk \-F/ '{print $3}') \-u admin \-p $(cat /opt/een/var/bridge/security/credentials.js | grep $i \-A8 | grep \-v ovcloud | grep admin \-A1 | tail \-1 | awk \-F '\\"' '{print $2}') \-f /opt/een/data/t3.6.1303.1004.88.1.8.2.4\_20231107"; done | bash  
* InHand Networks Device Manager

| Device Manager: [http://iot.inhandnetworks.com](http://iot.inhandnetworks.com/) |
| :---- |
| Login email: agarcia2+solar@een.com |
| Login password: 671522 SupportCTL wsl \--install (run together) curl \-Lo supportctl [http://asmodeus.aus1hub1.eencloud.com/api/v1/releases/releases/supportctl/linux/x64/latest/supportctl](http://asmodeus.aus1hub1.eencloud.com/api/v1/releases/releases/supportctl/linux/x64/latest/supportctl) chmod \+x supportctl sudo mv ./supportctl /usr/local/bin https://askubuntu.com/questions/1421805/error-2147942402-0x80070002-when-launching-ubuntu-18-04-in-wsl |

* **FFPROBE (in container)**  
  * ffprobe \-rtsp\_transport tcp \-loglevel repeat+level+verbose \-v debug "\<RTSpstream\>"  
    * ffprobe \-rtsp\_transport tcp \-loglevel repeat+level+verbose \-v debug "media/video1"  
  * ffprobe \-hide\_banner \-v debug "\<RTSPstream\>"  
  * ffprobe "rtsp://\<admin\>:\<password\>@\<IP\>:554/\<rtsp stream\>"  
    * ffprobe "rtsp://Admin:V8SRSecJnCwoORD@rtsp://10.1.3.33/media/video1?g726=rfc3551”

Model:IPC6312LR-AX4-VG

Firmware:CIPC-B2302.9.10.230928

GUID:00010010-0001-1020-8000-c4790577ad4e

**User:** kiran+aggregate@een.com

**Pin:** 959318

**Bridge Serial:** EEN-BR420-132425

**Bridge ESN:** 10061145	  
—-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------  
**Summary: watchdog.c Crashes on Bridge App 3.25.0** 

* **Description:**  
  * Infrequent crashes over the last few days with a reason of watchdog.c:83  
  * CPU is spiking anywhere from 70-100%  
  * Temps hover at a minimum of 35C (unit is well above the normal/expected operating temperature range)

**Troubleshooting:**

- 


**Expected Result:** To understand what’s causing these crashes in order to eliminate/remedy them for customer.  
---

User ID: 1339813  
Partner ID: 1530  
Camera ID: 2112290 & 2112282  
Cluster ID: 4  
PNP/Manually added camera: PNP (both)  
Platform (EECM/EENuboCam): Prosegur   
Client (Webapp/Mobile): Webapp  
Date issue reported: Oct 13th, 2024  
User link: https://dealer.cameramanager.com/\#/user/1727/1001675/1001675/support  
User Email: mic911e+ecu\_humanitas\_01@omnicloud.mx

Description: Customer is unable to pull live video using HLS via API. Customer is using the following HLS link:

 "hlsHttps": "https://ca012.cameramanager.com:443/stream/hls/getPlaylist?camera\_id=529111"
